terms.definition: When one half of a shape or figure is the exact mirror image of the other half. A shape with symmetry looks balanced and matching on both sides.
terms.exampleUsage: A butterfly has symmetry because both its wings look the same.

terms.definition: An imaginary line that divides a shape into two halves that are exact mirror images of each other. It is also called a mirror line or fold line.
terms.exampleUsage: A rectangle has 2 lines of symmetry - one horizontal and one vertical.

terms.definition: The reflection of a shape or object as seen in a mirror. In a mirror image, left and right sides are swapped.
terms.exampleUsage: When you look in a mirror, your right hand appears as the left hand in the mirror image.

terms.definition: A shape or object that has at least one line of symmetry. Both halves match when folded along the line of symmetry.
terms.exampleUsage: A circle is symmetrical because you can fold it along any line through the centre and both halves match.

terms.definition: A shape or object that does not have any line of symmetry. You cannot fold it so that both halves match exactly.
terms.exampleUsage: The letter J is asymmetrical because no fold line can make both halves match.

terms.definition: The image of a shape that appears on the other side of a line of symmetry, like looking in a mirror.
terms.exampleUsage: When a tree stands by a still lake, its reflection in the water shows symmetry.

terms.definition: The line along which you fold a paper to check for symmetry. If both halves match exactly when folded, the fold line is a line of symmetry.
terms.exampleUsage: Fold the heart shape along the middle. The fold line is its line of symmetry.

terms.definition: Another name for the line of symmetry. If you place a mirror on this line, the reflection completes the shape.
terms.exampleUsage: Place a mirror on the mirror line of half a circle and you will see a full circle.

terms.definition: A line of symmetry that goes across (left to right). The top half and bottom half are mirror images.
terms.exampleUsage: The letter B has a horizontal line of symmetry - the top half mirrors the bottom half.

terms.definition: A line of symmetry that goes up and down. The left half and right half are mirror images.
terms.exampleUsage: The letter A has a vertical line of symmetry - the left half mirrors the right half.

terms.definition: A figure or drawing that can be divided into two matching halves by at least one line of symmetry.
terms.exampleUsage: A rangoli pattern is a symmetric figure because it looks the same on all sides.

terms.definition: Drawing the missing half of a symmetric shape using the line of symmetry as a guide so that both halves match.
terms.exampleUsage: Given half a flower and a line of symmetry, complete the figure by drawing the mirror image on the other side.
